wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Momento Client Frontend project overview. Explain the premium landing page purpose, target audience for special event services (weddings, seserahan, mahar), and business objectives. Document the project's positioning as a high-end digital showcase platform. Include the brand identity, visual aesthetics, and core value proposition. Provide context about the special event industry focus and how this frontend serves as a digital ambassador for Momento's services. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and highlight the premium positioning throughout.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Primitives
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the UI primitive components in the Momento Client Frontend. Detail the Navbar component's fixed positioning, scroll-aware styling effects, and mobile-responsive hamburger menu implementation. Document the Footer component's multi-column layout, contact information display, and information architecture. Explain the component props, state management, event handlers, and styling approaches. Include examples of customization options, accessibility features, and integration patterns with the overall layout system. Address responsive design considerations and cross-browser compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dynamic Opening Section
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the dynamic opening section component. Explain the typing animation implementation using React hooks (useState, useEffect), the character-by-character text display logic, deletion cycle management, and timing controls. Document the floating WhatsApp integration with hover effects, the decorative bottom elements, and the responsive design considerations. Include code examples showing the animation state machine, customization options for text content and timing, and integration patterns with the main page layout.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Momento Client Frontend. Include detailed development environment setup instructions, prerequisite knowledge requirements (React, Next.js, Node.js), and step-by-step installation process. Document all supported package managers (npm, yarn, pnpm, bun) with their respective commands. Explain the project structure overview, key directories, and how to run the development server. Include troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Provide clear guidance for beginners while maintaining technical accuracy.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the feature components that implement the core services and landing page functionality. Document each component's purpose, props interface, state management, and integration patterns. Cover the dynamic typing animation in OpeningSection, the WhySection feature cards, service showcase components for Seserahan and Mahar, digital invitation section, highlight/portfolio section, testimonials display, and promotional banner. Explain component composition patterns, data flow between components, and reusability strategies. Include examples of customization, styling approaches, and performance considerations for each feature component.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Cards Section
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the why choose us feature cards section. Document the card layout implementation, feature presentation patterns, and content organization strategy. Explain the component structure, prop handling, and responsive design considerations. Include examples of feature card customization, content management approaches, and integration with the overall landing page flow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack & Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive technology stack documentation for the Momento Client Frontend. Detail the core technologies including Next.js 16 with App Router, React 19, and Tailwind CSS v4. Document all major dependencies including lucide-react for premium icons, Google Fonts integration, and React Compiler for performance optimization. Explain the build configuration, ESLint setup, and PostCSS configuration. Include version compatibility information, performance implications of each technology choice, and rationale behind the tech stack selection. Address development tools, optimization strategies, and integration patterns used throughout the project.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Composition
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the page composition architecture in the Next.js application. Explain how app/page.js orchestrates all feature components and serves as the main landing page container. Document the relationship between layout.js and page.js, including how the root layout provides global styling, fonts, and metadata while page.js manages the specific content composition. Detail the component hierarchy, prop passing strategies, and how different sections are integrated to create the cohesive landing page experience. Include examples of component ordering, conditional rendering, and performance optimization techniques used in the page composition.

wiki_catalogs.name: Seserahan Rental Showcase
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the seserahan rental showcase component. Detail the presentation format for rental items, image gallery implementation, pricing display, and service description layouts. Explain the component's content management approach, responsive design patterns, and integration with the overall service showcase. Include examples of customizing item displays, managing content arrays, and implementing interactive elements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed component architecture documentation for the Momento Client Frontend. Explain the hierarchical component structure with root layout, page composition, and reusable UI primitives. Document the separation between UI components (Navbar, Footer) and feature components (landing sections). Detail the component composition patterns, prop passing strategies, and state management approaches. Include the relationship between layout.js and page.js, component dependency chains, and reusability patterns. Provide examples of component integration, customization options, and extension points for adding new features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mahar Framing Display
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the mahar framing display component. Explain the framing presentation logic, product showcase layouts, and customization options for different mahar items. Document the component's implementation patterns, content organization strategies, and responsive design considerations. Include examples of adding new mahar items, managing inventory displays, and integrating with the overall service architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Sections Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the service sections implementation in the Momento Client Frontend. Detail each service showcase component including the dynamic opening section with typing animation, why choose us feature cards, seserahan rental presentation, mahar framing display, digital invitation section, portfolio/highlight showcase, testimonial system, and promotional banners. Explain the implementation patterns, interactive elements, content management approaches, and responsive design considerations for each section. Include code examples, customization options, and integration patterns with the overall page structure.

wiki_catalogs.name: Digital Invitation Section
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the digital invitation section component. Document the interactive preview functionality, template selection mechanisms, customization options, and user engagement features. Explain the component's state management, form handling, and integration with external services. Include examples of invitation customization workflows, preview generation, and responsive design patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling & Theming Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed styling and theming documentation for the Momento Client Frontend. Explain the Tailwind CSS v4 implementation, theme system configuration, and design token organization. Document the gold-themed aesthetic, typography hierarchy with three distinct font families, color palette management, and responsive design principles. Detail the global styling approach, component-level styling patterns, and customization options. Include guidelines for maintaining visual consistency, extending the theme system, and implementing dark/light mode variations if applicable. Provide examples of styling best practices and common customization scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Portfolio/Highlight Section
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the portfolio/highlight section component. Detail the image gallery implementation, portfolio item presentation, filtering mechanisms, and responsive grid layouts. Explain the component's content management approach, lazy loading strategies, and integration with the overall page structure. Include examples of adding new portfolio items, implementing filtering systems, and optimizing performance for large image collections.

wiki_catalogs.name: Navigation & Routing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive navigation and routing documentation for the Next.js App Router implementation. Detail the root layout configuration, page structure, and navigation component implementation. Explain the scroll-aware navbar functionality, route handling patterns, and integration with the overall page composition. Document the navigation data structure, active state management, and responsive navigation behavior. Include examples of adding new routes, customizing navigation behavior, and implementing advanced routing patterns. Address performance considerations and SEO implications of the routing strategy.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testimonial System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the testimonial system component. Document the testimonial carousel implementation, rating display logic, client feedback presentation, and social proof integration. Explain the component's state management for testimonials, automatic rotation controls, and responsive design patterns. Include examples of adding new testimonials, managing client photos, and implementing interactive elements like ratings and reviews.

wiki_catalogs.name: Image Optimization & Asset Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for image optimization and asset management in the Momento Client Frontend. Explain the Next.js Image component usage, asset organization strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Document the asset directory structure with images organized by service categories (extras, mahar-items, seserahan-items, testimonies, undangan-items), icon management, and optimization settings. Include best practices for image sizing, format selection, lazy loading implementation, and responsive image handling. Address asset delivery optimization, CDN considerations, and maintenance strategies for large media libraries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Promotional Banner
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the extra promotional banner component. Explain the banner positioning, promotional content display, call-to-action button implementation, and seasonal promotion handling. Document the component's responsive design considerations, animation effects, and integration with the overall page flow. Include examples of customizing promotional messages, managing banner visibility, and implementing promotional campaigns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines & Best Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines documentation for the Momento Client Frontend. Detail code organization standards, component development patterns, and architectural best practices. Document the ESLint configuration, TypeScript/JavaScript settings, and PostCSS setup for consistent code quality. Include guidelines for component naming conventions, file organization, state management patterns, and performance optimization. Address testing strategies, code review processes, and contribution workflows. Provide examples of proper implementation patterns and common pitfalls to avoid. Include guidelines for maintaining code consistency across the team and scaling development efforts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Production
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and production documentation for the Momento Client Frontend. Detail the build process optimization, deployment strategies using Vercel, and production performance considerations. Document the Next.js build configuration, environment variables management, and optimization settings for production. Include deployment workflows, CI/CD considerations, and monitoring strategies. Address performance monitoring, error tracking, and maintenance procedures for production environments. Provide guidance on scaling considerations, security best practices, and troubleshooting production issues. Include rollback procedures and emergency response protocols.

wiki_overview.content:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me create the analysis based on the information gathered.

<blog>

# Momento Client Frontend - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Momento Client Frontend is a premium web application designed to showcase and market special event services including seserahan (wedding reception), mahar (wedding gifts), and digital invitations. The platform serves as a client-facing web portal that emphasizes premium user experience, stunning visual presentation, and seamless navigation for Momento's special moments services.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Premium Experience**: Deliver a luxurious, gold-themed aesthetic that reflects the premium nature of wedding services
- **Service Showcase**: Effectively present all Momento services through engaging landing page sections
- **Conversion Optimization**: Guide users through clear pathways to pricing and booking services
- **Brand Consistency**: Maintain pixel-perfect alignment with Figma designs across all components

### Target Audience
- Engaged couples seeking premium wedding services
- Event planners looking for high-quality vendor solutions
- Budget-conscious consumers seeking premium experiences at competitive prices

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The application follows Next.js 16 App Router architecture with a clean, modular structure:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Root Layout] --> B[Navbar]
    A --> C[Main Content]
    A --> D[Footer]
    
    C --> E[Opening Section]
    C --> F[Why Section]
    C --> G[Seserahan Section]
    C --> H[Mahar Section]
    C --> I[Invitation Section]
    C --> J[Highlight Section]
    C --> K[Testimony Section]
    C --> L[Extra Banner]
    
    M[UI Components] --> N[Navbar]
    M --> O[Footer]
    P[Feature Components] --> Q[OpeningSection]
    P --> R[WhySection]
    P --> S[SeserahanSection]
    P --> T[MaharSection]
    P --> U[InvitationSection]
    P --> V[HighlightSection]
    P --> W[TestimonySection]
    P --> X[ExtraBanner]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Component Composition**: Modular UI components with clear separation of concerns
- **Theme-Based Styling**: Centralized color palette using gold (#D4AF37) as primary accent
- **Typography Hierarchy**: Three distinct font families (Cinzel for headings, Inter for body, Montserrat for navigation)
- **State Management**: Client-side state for interactive elements like navbar scroll effects and typing animations

### System Relationships
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Browser] --> B[Next.js App Router]
    B --> C[Root Layout]
    C --> D[UI Components]
    C --> E[Feature Components]
    D --> F[Navbar]
    D --> G[Footer]
    E --> H[Landing Sections]
    H --> I[Opening Animation]
    H --> J[Service Showcases]
    H --> K[Testimonials]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Root Layout** (`app/layout.js`): Centralizes global styling, fonts, and metadata configuration
- **Home Page** (`app/page.js`): Orchestrates all landing page sections in optimal user flow order

### Core Modules
- **Navigation System** (`components/ui/Navbar.js`): Fixed-position navigation with scroll-aware styling and responsive design
- **Hero Animation** (`components/features/landing/OpeningSection.js`): Dynamic text typing effect with professional gold styling
- **Service Showcase** (`components/features/landing/WhySection.js`): Feature cards highlighting service benefits
- **Footer Component** (`components/ui/Footer.js`): Multi-column information architecture with contact details

### Configuration Approach
- **Next.js Configuration** (`next.config.mjs`): React Compiler optimization and image remote pattern configuration
- **Global Styling** (`app/globals.css`): Tailwind CSS v4 with custom theme variables and gradient-based animations
- **Font Management** (`app/layout.js`): Google Fonts integration with CSS custom properties for optimal performance

### External Dependencies
- **React & Next.js**: Core framework for server-side rendering and modern React features
- **Lucide React**: Premium icon library for consistent visual elements
- **Tailwind CSS v4**: Utility-first CSS framework with advanced theming capabilities
- **Next Font**: Optimized font loading for improved performance

### Integration Points
- **Image Optimization**: Next.js Image component for all media assets with automatic optimization
- **Font Loading**: Google Fonts integration with fallback mechanisms
- **External Services**: WhatsApp integration for customer support

### Component Relationship Diagram
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Root Layout] --> B[Navbar]
    A --> C[Page Content]
    A --> D[Footer]
    
    C --> E[OpeningSection]
    C --> F[WhySection]
    C --> G[SeserahanSection]
    C --> H[MaharSection]
    C --> I[InvitationSection]
    C --> J[HighlightSection]
    C --> K[TestimonySection]
    C --> L[ExtraBanner]
    
    M[UI Primitives] --> B
    M --> D
    N[Feature Components] --> E
    N --> F
    N --> G
    N --> H
    N --> I
    N --> J
    N --> K
    N --> L
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Dynamic Hero Animation**: Typing text effect that showcases service offerings with smooth transitions
- **Responsive Navigation**: Scroll-aware navbar with mobile-friendly hamburger menu
- **Service Showcase**: Grid-based presentation of premium services with professional imagery
- **Interactive Elements**: Hover states, transitions, and micro-interactions throughout the interface
- **Gold Theme**: Consistent gold accent color scheme that conveys luxury and premium quality

### Implementation Highlights
- **Animation System** (`components/features/landing/OpeningSection.js`): Custom typing animation with configurable speed and loop behavior
- **Styling Architecture** (`app/globals.css`): Advanced gradient effects, custom animations, and responsive design utilities
- **Performance Optimization**: React Compiler enabled for improved runtime performance
- **Accessibility**: Proper semantic HTML structure with ARIA considerations

### Feature Architecture Diagram
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> HeroAnimation: Text Typing
    HeroAnimation --> ServiceShowcase: Animation Complete
    ServiceShowcase --> Navigation: User Interaction
    Navigation --> Pricing: Click Action
    Navigation --> Services: Scroll Down
    Services --> Testimonials: View Reviews
    Testimonials --> Contact: WhatsApp Click
    Contact --> [*]: Booking Complete
    
    HeroAnimation --> HeroAnimation: Loop Restart
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
Momento Client Frontend demonstrates excellent Next.js 16 implementation with a focus on premium user experience. The architecture successfully balances performance optimization with rich visual presentation, utilizing modern React features like Server Components and client-side state management effectively.

### Architectural Strengths
- **Clean Component Structure**: Clear separation between UI primitives and feature components
- **Performance Optimization**: React Compiler integration and efficient image handling
- **Visual Consistency**: Unified gold theme with professional typography hierarchy
- **Mobile Responsiveness**: Thoughtful responsive design with appropriate breakpoints

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **State Management**: Consider implementing a centralized state management solution for complex interactions
- **Testing Strategy**: Integrate comprehensive testing suite including component and integration tests
- **SEO Optimization**: Enhance meta tag management and structured data implementation
- **Performance Monitoring**: Implement performance monitoring tools for production insights

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement Testing**: Add Jest/React Testing Library for component testing coverage
2. **Enhance State Management**: Consider Zustand or Context API for complex state scenarios
3. **Performance Metrics**: Integrate Lighthouse CI for automated performance monitoring
4. **Accessibility Audit**: Conduct comprehensive accessibility testing and WCAG compliance verification

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Feature Expansion**: Develop booking system and user dashboard components
- **Content Management**: Implement headless CMS integration for dynamic content management
- **Analytics Integration**: Add comprehensive analytics tracking for user behavior insights
- **Internationalization**: Prepare infrastructure for multi-language support

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [app/layout.js](app/layout.js)
- [app/page.js](app/page.js)
- [components/ui/Navbar.js](components/ui/Navbar.js)
- [components/features/landing/OpeningSection.js](components/features/landing/OpeningSection.js)
- [components/features/landing/WhySection.js](components/features/landing/WhySection.js)
- [components/ui/Footer.js](components/ui/Footer.js)
- [next.config.mjs](next.config.mjs)
- [app/globals.css](app/globals.css)
- [DOCS_OVERVIEW.md](DOCS_OVERVIEW.md)

</blog>
